licensing_attribution: CAIQ question content adapted from the Cloud Security Alliance (CSA) Consensus Assessments Initiative Questionnaire (CAIQ) v4.0.3, licensed CC BY 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). Original publication: https://cloudsecurityalliance.org/research/cloud-controls-matrix/. The Evidentia caiq-full template is an expanded ~50-question subset covering all 17 CAIQ control domains with multiple representative questions per domain. Operators wanting the full 245-question CAIQ should download from CSA + use the `--from-template <licensed-xlsx>` BYO ingestion path once it ships in a follow-up Evidentia release.
description: Expanded CAIQ v4.0.3 representative questionnaire with multiple representative questions per CSA control domain. Complementary to caiq-lite (which is a one-question-per-domain subset).

questions.question_text: Do you provide independent third-party audit reports (SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, FedRAMP, or equivalent) covering the services you provide?
Do you maintain an internal audit program and document non-conformities and remediation actions?
Are audit reports available to customers under NDA on request, including the scope, controls tested, and any exceptions?
Do you follow a documented secure SDLC, including security requirements gathering, threat modeling, secure coding standards, and pre-release security testing?
Do you perform application-level penetration tests at least annually and remediate findings?
Do you implement input validation, output encoding, and parameterized queries to mitigate OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities?
Do you have a documented business continuity and disaster recovery plan, tested at least annually, with results reported to leadership?
What are your stated and tested Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO) for the services covered by this questionnaire?
Are backups encrypted, tested regularly via restore exercises, and stored in a geographically separate region from production?
Do you have a formal change management process requiring documented approvals, security review, and rollback plans for all production changes?
Are configuration baselines documented and continuously monitored for drift in production environments?
Do you classify customer data and apply differential controls (access, encryption, retention) by classification level?
Do you have documented data retention and disposal procedures including secure destruction (cryptographic erasure or NIST 800-88 sanitization) at end-of-life?
Have you implemented a privacy program with a designated DPO/privacy officer, and do you respond to data subject access requests within regulatory timeframes?
questions.notes: Adapted from CAIQ DSP-09 / privacy controls.

questions.question_text: Do you operate from datacenters with documented physical security controls (perimeter, badge access, multi-factor entry, video surveillance)?
Do you maintain visitor logs and accompanied-access policies for non-employee datacenter visitors?
Is customer data encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher, and at rest using AES-256 or equivalent industry-standard cryptography?
Do you operate a key management system with documented procedures for key rotation, escrow, and destruction (HSM-backed where applicable)?
Do you offer customer-managed encryption keys (BYOK) or hold-your-own-key (HYOK) for sensitive customer workloads?
Do you maintain a documented information security policy approved by senior leadership and reviewed at least annually?
Do you operate a formal risk management program including risk assessments, treatment plans, and exception tracking with executive accountability?
Have you appointed a senior information security leader (CISO equivalent) with direct reporting to executive leadership?
Do you perform background checks on personnel handling customer data, scoped appropriately to data sensitivity and applicable regulations?
Do all personnel sign confidentiality agreements covering customer data handling at hiring and review them annually?
Do you provide annual security awareness training to all personnel and role-specific training to those handling customer data?
Is multi-factor authentication enforced for all privileged access and customer-facing administrative consoles?
Do you enforce least-privilege access controls and conduct documented periodic access reviews (quarterly or more frequently)?
Are privileged accounts monitored continuously (session recording, behavioral analytics, alerts on anomalies)?
Do you support federated identity (SAML 2.0, OIDC) for customer single-sign-on integration?
Are tenant environments logically isolated (network segmentation, dedicated identity boundary, separate encryption keys per tenant where applicable)?
Do you operate network firewalls, IDS/IPS, and continuous network monitoring for the infrastructure hosting customer workloads?
Are hypervisors and orchestration platforms patched within documented SLAs after vendor security advisories?

If your service includes mobile applications, do you implement mobile application security testing including platform-specific guidelines (iOS App Transport Security, Android Network Security Config)?
Are mobile devices accessing customer data managed via MDM with enforced encryption, remote-wipe, and minimum OS version policies?
Do you have a documented incident response plan with defined severity tiers, stakeholder roles, communication trees, and exercise it at least annually?
What is your committed customer-notification SLA for security incidents involving customer data (e.g., '72 hours from confirmation')?
Do you preserve forensic evidence (logs, memory captures, disk images) after incidents and provide it to customers under a documented forensic-cooperation policy?
Have you experienced a security incident involving customer data in the past 24 months that triggered customer notification under regulatory requirements?
Do you maintain a documented inventory of subprocessors / 4th parties handling customer data and notify customers of material changes?
Do contracts with subprocessors flow down equivalent security and data-protection obligations from your customer contracts?
Do you assess subprocessor security posture at onboarding and at least annually thereafter (questionnaires, audits, or rating-tool evidence)?
Do you perform vulnerability scans on production infrastructure and applications at least monthly with remediation SLAs by severity?
Do you operate a coordinated vulnerability disclosure program and respond to external researcher reports within published SLAs?
Do you operate endpoint protection (anti-malware, EDR) on systems that touch customer data, with continuous monitoring and central alerting?
Do you perform static (SAST) and dynamic (DAST) security testing as part of your CI/CD pipeline?
Do you maintain and ship Software Bills of Materials (SBOMs) for the services covered by this questionnaire?
questions.notes: Adapted from CAIQ AIS-07; aligns to CISA SBOM expectations.

questions.question_text: Are data processing locations and customer data residency commitments documented and contractually committed?
Are user accounts disabled within documented SLAs (typically 24 hours) upon termination or role change?
Do you implement DDoS protection on customer-facing endpoints?
Do you log all access to customer data (read AND write), retain logs for at least 12 months, and make logs available for customer review under a documented procedure?
Do you carry sufficient cyber insurance coverage to address customer breach-notification costs and indemnification obligations?
questions.notes: Free-text follow-up requested for coverage limits.
